analysis_metadata.protocol: Matter R1.4 Section 7.15 - Atomic Write Protocol
analysis_metadata.methodology: Formal FSM verification with specification citation

executive_summary.primary_concern: All-or-nothing atomicity guarantee violated - partial commits possible

critical_violations.violation_description: The FSM applies pending writes SEQUENTIALLY during commit (T031) but lacks a rollback mechanism when any write fails (T032). This allows partial commits where some writes succeed and others fail, violating the fundamental all-or-nothing atomicity guarantee.

attack_sequence.result: Enters T031 with N pending writes

specification_gap.quote_actual: For each write request in the list of pending writes, the server SHALL call the underlying storage layer... b. i. If any write fails to apply: Send a response... with status code FAILURE
specification_gap.quote_missing: NO EXPLICIT ROLLBACK REQUIREMENT - Spec says 'return FAILURE' but NOT 'undo previously applied writes'
specification_gap.interpretation: Specification describes SEQUENTIAL application ('for each') and failure reporting but omits rollback mechanism for atomicity

fsm_gap.required_action: Must undo writes 1..k before returning FAILURE

attack_scenario.description: Attacker commits transaction with 3 writes where write 3 will fail
preconditions: Client associated with Atomic Write State
Write 3 will fail (e.g., storage layer error, insufficient resources)
execution: T030: CommitWrite received, all validations pass
- NO ROLLBACK OF ATTR1 AND ATTR2
postconditions: VIOLATED: All-or-nothing guarantee - partial commit occurred
attack_scenario.impact: Client believes transaction failed but 2/3 writes committed

constraint_violation.property_claim: ∀ transaction T with writes W = {w1, w2, ..., wn}: (commit(T) = SUCCESS → ∀i: applied(wi)) ∧ (commit(T) = FAILURE → ∀i: ¬applied(wi))
constraint_violation.counterexample: Transaction T with W = {w1, w2, w3}, commit(T) = FAILURE, but applied(w1) ∧ applied(w2) ∧ ¬applied(w3)

acid_properties.consistency: VIOLATED - cluster state inconsistent with transaction semantics

security_implications: State corruption: Cluster left in undefined state
Constraint violations: Partial writes may violate collective constraints
Denial of service: Repeated partial commits could exhaust resources
Data integrity: Client-side rollback impossible without transaction history
impact_analysis.operational_impact: CRITICAL - requires cluster reset or manual intervention to fix inconsistent state

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit rollback requirement to Section 7.15.6.4 CommitWrite step 6.b: 'If any write fails to apply: (i) Undo all previously applied writes in reverse order, (ii) Send FAILURE response'
recommendations.rationale: Specification must mandate atomic semantics, not just describe sequential application

recommendations.recommendation: Add rollback action to T032: Before discard_atomic_write_state(), call rollback_successfully_applied_writes(list_of_applied_writes)
Require two-phase commit or transaction log in implementation: (1) Validate all writes, (2) Apply all writes with rollback capability, (3) Commit transaction
recommendations.rationale: Atomic application requires transactional storage layer

recommendations.recommendation: Rename apply_all_pending_writes_atomically() to apply_all_pending_writes_with_rollback() and update definition to include rollback logic
recommendations.rationale: Function name implies atomicity but implementation is sequential

critical_violations.violation_description: SAME ROOT CAUSE AS PROP_005. Property requires that if any write fails during application, the server returns FAILURE with detailed statuses. While the FSM correctly returns FAILURE (T032), it fails to rollback successfully applied writes, leaving the system in an inconsistent state.
critical_violations.relationship_to_prop_005: This is a consequence violation of PROP_005. The lack of rollback mechanism causes both properties to fail.
critical_violations.fsm_gap: Identical to PROP_005 - T032 missing rollback_successfully_applied_writes() action
critical_violations.recommendations: Same as PROP_005 - fix rollback mechanism

ambiguities.ambiguity_description: The function apply_shared_resource_expansion(request, status_list) may or may not update status_list by reference. The FSM definition does not specify whether status_list is modified in-place or returned as a new value.

possible_interpretations.behavior: status_list updated in-place, T009/T010 see updated statuses
status_list not updated, expansion statuses lost

ambiguities.impact: LOW - Most implementations would naturally use pass-by-reference for efficiency

recommendation.recommendation: Clarify function signature: apply_shared_resource_expansion(request, status_list_ref) → void OR apply_shared_resource_expansion(request, status_list) → expanded_status_list

design_flaws.flaw_description: The specification intentionally allows external commands (non-Atomic Write) to modify attributes that are part of an ongoing transaction. This is a race condition by design, not an FSM bug.

specification_intent.quote: Requests from other cluster commands SHALL NOT be rejected during a transaction

fsm_behavior.transition: T021 (external write modifies attribute with pending write)
fsm_behavior.mitigation: T029 (constraint validation at commit detects violations)
fsm_behavior.result: Transaction may fail at commit if external modification causes constraint violation

security_analysis.race_condition: Intentional - external writes can interleave with transactions
security_analysis.impact: MEDIUM - availability prioritized over strong isolation

design_flaws.verdict: NOT_AN_FSM_BUG - This is a specification-level design decision. The FSM correctly implements the specified behavior.

recommendation.recommendation: Add explicit warning in specification: 'This protocol provides READ_COMMITTED isolation, not SERIALIZABLE. External modifications during transactions are permitted and may cause commit failures.'

properties_holding.summary: These properties are correctly enforced by the FSM through appropriate guard conditions, action sequences, and function definitions.

overall_assessment.fsm_correctness: 95% - One critical atomicity violation, one ambiguity
overall_assessment.security_posture: CRITICAL_RISK - Partial commits break fundamental guarantees
overall_assessment.implementation_risk: HIGH - Without rollback mechanism, all implementations vulnerable to state corruption
top_priority_fixes: 1. Add rollback mechanism to specification (Section 7.15.6.4 CommitWrite)
2. Add rollback action to FSM transition T032
overall_assessment.validation_status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE - All 43 properties verified with specification citations
